topic: A neighbor wants to install a fence
raw_output:
[story 1]

The afternoon sun glinted off the freshly polished metal of my garden tools, a stark contrast to the knot tightening in my chest. Mr. Henderson was already on his lawn, surveying the property line with an air of proprietorship that grated on my nerves. I watched him pace, his shadow stretching long and accusatory across the patchy grass. Every time he glanced my way, it felt like an inventory, a silent assessment of my shortcomings. I gripped the handle of the spade so tightly my knuckles turned white. *Another imposition,* I thought, the resentment a slow, cold seep in my core. He wouldn't stop until that fence was erected, a physical barrier separating us, solidifying some invisible judgment he already held about my yard.

[story 2]

"Are you going to let this go on?" I asked, my voice sounding sharper than I intended, the sound catching in my throat like grit. He didn't even look up from the blueprint spread across his kitchen table. His response was a slow, deliberate sigh, the sound heavy with condescension. I stood there, arms crossed, the stiffness in my shoulders a physical manifestation of my mounting frustration. I could feel the heat rising in my neck, a flush that had nothing to do with the weather. This whole situation felt like a deliberate siege, and I was just the unwilling target. I wanted to shout, to tell him to leave me alone, but the words dissolved before they reached my lips, replaced by a dull, heavy resignation.

[story 3]

I meticulously straightened the wilting petunias, my movements overly precise, almost aggressive. When Mr. Henderson finally approached, holding a rolled-up schematic, I didn't offer a greeting. I simply met his gaze, my expression carefully neutral, a mask stretched taut over something much harder underneath. "The plans are ready," I stated, the words clipped and devoid of warmth. He beamed, a wide, unearned expression that made my jaw clench. I could feel a distinct tightness around my eyes, a sensation of being perpetually scrutinized. It was tiresome, this constant need to perform tolerance for someone whose intentions felt so transparently self-serving. I just wanted the whole tiresome affair to end.
